title: Make STARGATE_ENABLE_FUNDING_SYNC opt-in (disabled by default)
close_reason: Implemented: funding sync (and escort validation) is now opt-in only via STARGATE_ENABLE_FUNDING_SYNC=true. Reduces log spam from 'Funding sync cache miss' after direct PSBT + OP_RETURN donation flow made external proof refresh unnecessary for normal operation. Tests + build + vet green.

title: Align product image for hashlock commitment in PSBT builder when no donation amount is provided
description: Currently the PSBT builder and funding flow always use the original wish/proposal visible_pixel_hash for the hashlock commitment script (buildCommitmentScript / buildHashlockRedeemScript in bitcoin/psbt_builder.go). Per the refined model, when the payer provides no extra 'donation' component (only labor payment), the hashlock (if still created) should be based on the final delivered product image (the artifact with tasks inscribed via steganography) instead of the original wish image. This requires changes in the funding handler (server.go) and possibly deferring or updating the commitment at submission/approval time rather than only at funding time. Also update any related reconciliation and sweep logic.
close_reason: Implemented: product image hash used for hashlock when no donation. Added CommitmentSource tracking, product commitment_target, and stego reconciliation alignment.

title: visual regression after upgrading quantum css from v1.6.2 to v1.11.7.
description: App header search input field icon is overlapping with place holder text.  Dropdown menu has strange white or 3d borders.  Footer text is not vertically centered.  Footer is showing over Inscribe dialog.  Discover page log:col-span-2 isn't working.
close_reason: Fixed visual regressions from quantumcss upgrade. Added explicit grid column spans (col-span-1/2/3, md:col-span-*, lg:col-span-*), fixed search input padding for icon clearance with z-index, added dropdown box-shadow and border-radius, and ensured footer z-index is explicitly lower than modals.

title: Centralize all storage logic (MCP, auth, data, ingestion) into backend/storage with unified factory and STARGATE_STORAGE
notes: All 7 phases of the storage centralization refactoring complete and pushed on feature/single-binary. backend/storage is now the single authoritative package for Data, MCP/Store, Auth, Ingestion, Schema, Config, and Factory. STARGATE_STORAGE controls everything consistently. memory for debug/business logic, sqlite for durable single-binary (no hybrid). Epic closed.

title: Phase 1: Move Store interface definition from middleware/smart_contract/storage.go into storage/smart_contract/store.go + compatibility alias
notes: Phase 1 complete: Store interface + Err consolidated in storage/smart_contract/{store.go,errors.go}. Middleware now re-exports for compatibility. go build + go test + 'make' single-binary all succeeded. No runtime behavior change.

title: Phase 2: Introduce StorageConfig + NewAllStores factory in backend/storage for unified STARGATE_STORAGE-driven initialization of Data, MCP, Auth, and Ingestion stores
notes:
Phase 2 progress: StorageConfig + NewAllStores factory skeleton implemented in backend/storage/factory.go. AllStores unifies DataStorage, SmartContractStore, APIKey*, ChallengeStore, IngestionService, ContractCache. Switch on cfg.Type handles memory/sqlite/postgres/filesystem. go build + tests + 'make' (single-binary) all green. Ready for wiring in subsequent steps.
Migrated from feature/single-binary (was stuck in_progress). Verified on chore/refactor-cleanup: StorageConfig + LoadStorageConfigFromEnv + NewAllStores in backend/storage; initializeMCPComponents delegates to factory; phases 3–7 and epic stargate-ood already closed. No further work required.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete; NewAllStores factory shipped and later phases closed

title: Phase 3: Move IngestionService (and related types) from services/ingestion_service.go into storage/ingestion/ for full storage centralization
notes: Phase 3 complete: IngestionService fully moved into backend/storage/ingestion/service.go (canonical home). A minimal compatibility shim remains in services/ingestion_service.go so zero callers required import changes. Full build + 'make' single-binary + push succeeded. Storage package is now the owner of block data, smart-contract/MCP, auth, and ingestion.

title: Phase 4: Unify schema management across SQLite and PostgreSQL stores (smart_contract + ingestion)
notes: Phase 4 complete: Created storage/smart_contract/schema.go with GetMCPSchema(dialect) as the single source of truth. Both schema_manager.go (Postgres) and sqlite_store.go now delegate to it. Added GetIngestionSchema stub for future SQLite ingestion work. Massive reduction in duplicated CREATE TABLE strings.

title: Phase 5: Eliminate type assertions and brittle coupling on Store (ingestion_sync, SweepTaskStore, sync starters)
notes: Phase 5 complete: Added UpsertContractWithTasks to the core Store interface. Removed the dangerous *scstore.PGStore concrete type assertion in ingestion_sync.go (now uses the generic Store interface and works for all backends). Simplified SweepTaskStore handling in stargate_backend.go (all stores satisfy it). Build + make + tests green.

title: Phase 6: Add SQLiteDataStorage for block/inscription data layer + clarify memory/sqlite modes for single-binary vs tests
notes: Phase 6 complete: Added SQLiteDataStorage (block_scans table in SQLite) + wired it into NewAllStores when STARGATE_STORAGE=sqlite. memory mode remains filesystem+RAM (perfect for tests). sqlite mode now gives fully consistent durable embedded storage for blocks metadata + api_keys + mcp + ingestions. Clarified the memory vs sqlite semantics per user requirements.

title: Phase 7: Final cleanup — wire main/container to NewAllStores, remove duplicated init logic, set sqlite as recommended default, update docs, close epic
notes: Phase 7 (final cleanup) complete: initializeMCPComponents now delegates to the unified factory (massive duplication removed). Defaults in run_dev.sh + Dockerfile changed to sqlite. Clear documentation added that memory = debug/business logic, sqlite = durable single-binary, no hybrid supported. Epic ready to close.

title: Explore embedding SHA256 of sandbox tarball into product image for verifiable AI-generated binary provenance
description: In the context of the single-binary distribution model (downloadable runnable binary without requiring the full microservice stack), consider tarring the relevant sandbox/artifact state at submission time and embedding the SHA256 hash of that tarball into the final product image via steganography. This would provide a strong, bit-level verifiable 'signature' that ties the exact delivered binary/content back to the specific task and the AI generation process. Discuss implications for security, single-binary usability, reproducibility, and how it interacts with the existing visible_pixel_hash + hashlock + witness reconciliation system.
close_reason: Implemented: deterministic sandbox tarball SHA256 computed at submission, embedded in stego manifest. Includes HashSandboxDir/VerifySandboxHash utilities with full test coverage.
